system_description: Persian (Farsi) SOV disambiguation with nearly-lost case. Old Persian had 8 grammatical cases; Modern Persian retains only traces in pronouns (man/mara) and the object marker -ra (specific/definite direct objects). Word order SOV is relatively rigid. The ezafe construction (-e linking element) connects NP modifiers to heads. Verb agreement exists for person and number but is relatively simple compared to Arabic. The -ra object marker is the primary formal disambiguation signal for direct objects; without it, NP roles depend entirely on word order.
notes: PRE-REGISTERED prediction: k_eff ~3.2-4.4, higher than Hindi because case is nearly gone. The -ra marker covers specific/definite direct objects but leaves generic/indefinite objects unmarked. Word order carries most disambiguation load for those cases. Prediction: higher k_eff than Hindi (2.4-3.6) because of missing case richness. Falsifier: if -ra marker coverage is high enough in UD to produce strong case_density signal, Persian k_eff could be lower than predicted.
